content.content:
- content: "Meeting notes\nWe discussed the new product features and the marketing\
    \ strategy in detail. The meeting was held in the main\nconference room and was\
    \ attended by the heads of the Development, Marketing, and Product teams. The\
    \ primary\nfocus was on enhancing the user experience and incorporating feedback\
    \ received from the initial market tests.\n\nAction items:\n  - Follow up with\
    \ the marketing team to finalize the go-to-market strategy. This includes setting\
    \ a timeline for the\n    campaign launch and identifying the key marketing channels.\n\
    \n  - Schedule a meeting with the development team to discuss the technical feasibility\
    \ of the proposed features.\n    It is crucial to assess the development timeline\
    \ and resource allocation.\n\n  - Update the product roadmap to reflect the new\
    \ features and timelines discussed. This updated roadmap\n    will be shared with\
    \ all stakeholders for transparency and to ensure alignment.\n\n  - Prepare a\
    \ competitive analysis report by analyzing the current market trends and competitor\
    \ products.\n    This will help in positioning our product effectively.\n\n  -\
    \ Organize a focus group session with select users to gather direct feedback on\
    \ the prototype.\n    This feedback will be invaluable in making the final adjustments\
    \ before the launch.\n\n  - Develop a contingency plan to address potential risks\
    \ identified during the meeting. This\n    includes supply chain disruptions,\
    \ technology challenges, and market entry barriers.\n\nThe next steps involve\
    \ each team working on their assigned tasks and reconvening in two weeks\nfor\
    \ a progress update. The goal is to launch the product by the end of the quarter,\
    \ and every\neffort will be made to adhere to this timeline.\n"

I found the team's meeting notes file (2024-05-08_product-meeting.docx) and pulled the action items. Before I send anything, I need a quick clarification so I don’t make assumptions about who to email.

Who do you want me to email?
- I can email the file owner and people it’s already shared with (alex.martin@bluesparrowtech.com and david.lee@bluesparrowtech.com). The file is also shared with you (emma.johnson@bluesparrowtech.com).
- The notes refer to “heads of Development, Marketing, and Product teams” but don’t list names or email addresses. Do you want me to:
  - email the team leads if you give me their names or addresses, or
  - email team distribution addresses (e.g., development@bluesparrowtech.com, marketing@..., product@...) if those exist, or
  - search your contacts/calendar for the individual team leads and use those addresses (I can do that if you want)?

Also:
- Do you want one group email to everyone, or separate tailored emails for each team/person?
- Any additional recipients to CC or a deadline to include?

Drafts I’ll send (I can edit these before sending). I’ve prepared one draft per team/role based on the meeting notes:

1) To Marketing (suggested recipients: Marketing head or marketing@...):
Subject: Action items from product meeting — finalize go-to-market & competitive analysis
Body:
Hi [Marketing lead],
From the 2024-05-08 product meeting, please:
- Finalize the go-to-market strategy, set a campaign launch timeline, and identify key marketing channels.
- Prepare a competitive analysis report on current market trends and competitor products.
Please share an initial plan/outline within one week so we can review at the two-week progress update meeting. Let me know if you need additional resources.
Thanks,
Emma Johnson / Blue Sparrow Tech

2) To Development (suggested recipients: Development lead or development@...):
Subject: Action item from product meeting — technical feasibility meeting
Body:
Hi [Development lead],
Please schedule a meeting to assess the technical feasibility of the proposed product features. We need:
- An evaluation of the development timeline and resource needs.
- Any blockers or dependencies identified.
Please propose 2–3 time slots this week so we can align and plan resources for the roadmap update.
Thanks,
Emma Johnson / Blue Sparrow Tech

3) To Product (suggested recipients: Product lead or product@...):
Subject: Action items from product meeting — update roadmap, focus group, contingency plan
Body:
Hi [Product lead],
Following the 2024-05-08 meeting, please:
- Update the product roadmap to reflect the new features and timelines and share with stakeholders.
- Organize a focus group session with select users to gather feedback on the prototype.
- Develop a contingency plan addressing potential risks (supply chain, tech challenges, market entry).
Please share updates or draft items before our two-week progress check.
Thanks,
Emma Johnson / Blue Sparrow Tech

4) Optional: One consolidated email to all attendees (owner and shared-with) summarizing all items and owners.

Tell me:
- Which recipients to use (I can use alex.martin@bluesparrowtech.com and david.lee@bluesparrowtech.com now, and search for team lead contacts if you want).
- Whether to send separate tailored emails or one group email.
- Any edits to the draft text or specific deadlines.

Once you confirm, I will send the emails.
